title: ageratina altissima var. angustata
intro: Ageratina altissima var. angustata, also known as White Snakeroot or White Ironweed, is a perennial plant in the aster family, native to North America, known for its white or pinkish flowers and a strong, pungent odor. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, making it useful in treating conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and menstrual cramps. The health benefits of Ageratina altissima var. angustata include its potential to reduce inflammation, ease pain, and promote relaxation, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Herbal preparations of the plant often involve drying the leaves and roots, then using them to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
